answers: Nuclear energy is generated by splitting atoms (fission) in a reactor, which produces enormous heat used to generate electricity. It's one of the most powerful energy sources available.

Nuclear power provides about 10% of the world's electricity. It produces massive amounts of energy with very low carbon emissions, though waste management and safety remain challenges.
Nuclear energy uses uranium or plutonium fuel in reactors to generate heat through fission. It's very efficient — a small amount of nuclear fuel produces enormous energy compared to fossil fuels.
